challenges.title: Interactive Input with getline
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that uses getline to read the user's full name (a string with spaces) and cin to read their birth year (an int). Display a formatted greeting. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Include the string header
- Declare a string variable for the name
- Use getline to read the full name
- Use cin to read the birth year
- Display a formatted message with cout
challenges.test_code:
assert '#include <string>' in cpp, 'Should include the string header'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Should use getline to read a full line of input'
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Should use cin for numeric input'
assert 'string ' in cpp, 'Should declare a string variable'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should display output with cout'
hints: Use: getline(cin, name); to read a full line including spaces
If you use cin before getline, call cin.ignore() first to clear the newline

challenges.title: Constants, Casting, and Expressions
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that defines a const for the tax rate (e.g., 0.08), reads a purchase price, uses static_cast to convert between types, and calculates the total with tax. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Define at least one const variable
- Use static_cast for a type conversion
- Calculate a result using an arithmetic expression
- Display the result with cout
challenges.test_code:
assert 'const ' in cpp, 'Should define a const variable'
assert 'static_cast' in cpp, 'Should use static_cast for type conversion'
assert '*' in cpp or '+' in cpp, 'Should use arithmetic expressions'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Should display the result'
assert 'double' in cpp or 'float' in cpp, 'Should use a floating-point type for the calculation'
hints: Define a constant like: const double TAX_RATE = 0.08;
Use static_cast<double>(intVar) to convert an int to a double
